channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Treasury CS Mbadi Warns Matatu Strike Could Worsen Fuel Crisis and Economy
item.description: Treasury Cabinet Secretary John Mbadi has raised alarm over the ongoing matatu strike in Kenya, saying it risks further hurting an already fragile economy. The strike started on Monday as transport op...

item.title: VD Satheesan sworn in as Kerala Chief Minister with Congress-led cabinet
item.description: VD Satheesan has officially taken charge as the Chief Minister of Kerala, marking the return of a Congress-led government in the state after a brief post-election delay. The swearing-in ceremony was c...

item.title: PNP prepares for possible protests as Senate begins impeachment proceedings for VP Sara Duterte
item.description: Police are on heightened alert as the Senate opens its impeachment court to hear the case against Vice President Sara Duterte. Demonstrations are expected from supporters and critics alike, with the P...

item.title: Randeep sets sights on representing Tanzania in global rally competitions
item.description: Randeep, the Tanzanian rally driver, has expressed his strong commitment to representing Tanzania at the international rally stage. He emphasized that his goal is not only to compete but also to make ...

item.title: CJ Kirst Wins NLL Championship, Building on a Distinguished Lacrosse Career
item.description: CJ Kirst, a standout lacrosse player for the Toronto Rock, recently helped his team secure their seventh National Lacrosse League (NLL) championship with a 12-7 victory over the Halifax Thunderbirds, ...

item.title: Traffic officer helps family reach hospital in time for premature birth
item.description: In a touching story from Belhar, the Botes family reunited with Officer Blaauw, the traffic officer who helped them reach Life Vincent Pallotti Hospital when Merle Botes went into premature labour at ...

item.title: The Life and Fashion Photography of Arnaud de Rosnay in the 1960s
item.description: Arnaud de Rosnay (1946–1984) was a French aristocrat, photographer, and extreme windsurfer whose life combined glamour and daring adventure. Initially making a name for himself as a fashion photograph...

item.title: Protests and Fuel Strike Cause Major Disruptions on Thika Road, Leaving Commuters Stranded
item.description: On Monday morning, Thika Road became a scene of major disruption as protesters set tyres ablaze, blocking sections of the busy highway and forcing motorists to make abrupt U-turns. This unrest left co...

item.title: Adamawa State Ensures Proper Accommodation for Pilgrims in Makkah, Viral Claims Debunked
item.description: The Adamawa State Government don dey assure say all pilgrims wey dey for 2026 Hajj from di state don get proper accommodation for Makkah. According to di Adamawa Pilgrims Welfare Commission, over 1,00...

item.title: Kozak Financial Group Offers Expert Investment Guidance for Albertans
item.description: Kozak Financial Group, with nearly three decades of experience, provides Albertans with reliable, income-oriented investment strategies. The firm focuses on creating long-term financial plans that pri...

item.title: Spider-Man Noir series premieres May 27 on Prime Video with all 8 episodes
item.description: The release of the live-action Spider-Man Noir series has finally been confirmed. The series will first air all eight episodes on MGM+ in the U.S. on May 25, 2026, giving subscribers a two-day early a...

item.title: Australia Outlaws Neo-Nazi Group Following Bondi Beach Massacre
item.description: Australia has officially banned the neo-Nazi group formerly known as the National Socialist Network, also called White Australia, under its recently enacted hate group legislation. The law, introduced...

item.title: Carlos Yulo Achieves Significant Gymnastics Victory in France
item.description: Filipino gymnast Carlos Yulo recently marked a significant achievement in his international gymnastics career by winning a notable competition in France. Yulo, known for his impressive skills and cons...

item.title: Karen Carpenter on Stage: Highlights from the 1970s and Early 1980s
item.description: Karen Anne Carpenter, born March 2, 1950, was an iconic American musician best known as the lead vocalist and drummer of the Carpenters, alongside her older brother Richard. Renowned for her distincti...

item.title: BBNaija Season 11 Physical Auditions Scheduled for Lagos, Abuja, and Enugu
item.description: MultiChoice Nigeria don announce say auditions for Big Brother Naija Season 11 go start on May 22. This year, dem go run the auditions physically, and anybody wey wan apply suppose register from May 1...

item.title: UNIS Members Share Insights on Their Growth and Upcoming Plans
item.description: Members of the K-pop group UNIS recently reflected on their journey since forming, sharing their experiences, challenges, and personal growth as a group. They expressed gratitude for the support of th...

item.title: Guide to fostering emotional maturity in children and adults
item.description: Lindsay C Gibson, author of the bestselling book Adult Children of Emotionally Immature Parents, has released a new guide titled How to Raise an Emotionally Mature Child. The psychologist explains tha...

item.title: Intimate Street Portraits Capturing Vulnerability in Washington Square Park
item.description: Amy Horowitz’s photobook, 'A Walk in the Park? — Street Portraits from NYC,' is a deeply human collection of street portraits taken over five years in Washington Square Park and the West Village. Horo...

item.title: Two policemen killed after dumper truck hits parked police vehicle on Karachi’s M9 Motorway
item.description: Two police officials lost their lives in a tragic road accident on Monday on the M9 Motorway near Karachi, within the limits of Gadap City police station. According to police officials, a dumper truck...

item.title: Investigation reveals UK firms’ bank accounts used to facilitate Channel migrant crossings
item.description: A BBC investigation has uncovered that people smugglers are using UK-registered businesses to facilitate illegal crossings of the English Channel. Over three months, undercover researchers discovered ...
